citations.description: test_git_metrics_registered_with_labels: checks lens and source but never confidence; uses `assert present` (any match) not `assert present == named` (all four required)
MetricSpec built with confidence='high' in _spec() — implementation is correct but test does not assert it

findings.criterion: each of the four metric ids (module_size_distribution, cap_change_events, churn, refactor_to_addition_ratio) is present in REGISTRY with lens="code_health", source="git", confidence="high" — asserted by iterating REGISTRY and checking .id/.lens/.source (so the assertion fails if a metric is unregistered or mislabeled, not merely that is_authoritative("git") is trivially True).
findings.detail: The test test_git_metrics_registered_with_labels (test_git_metrics_heldout.py:92-103) iterates REGISTRY and checks .lens and .source for the registered git metrics, but never asserts .confidence == 'high'. The AC explicitly requires confidence="high" to be asserted. Additionally, the test uses `assert present` (truthy, satisfied by any single matching metric) rather than asserting all four metric ids are present (e.g. `assert present == named`), meaning the assertion does not fail if up to three of the four metrics are unregistered. The implementation in git_metrics.py does register all four with confidence='high', but the test does not verify this as the AC requires.
findings.remediation: Add `assert git_specs[mid].confidence == 'high'` inside the `for mid in present:` loop in test_git_metrics_registered_with_labels. Also change `assert present` to `assert present == named, f'missing: {named - present}'` to enforce all four ids are registered.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
